613jvo8hikdmuscksfb3oqbk29.summary: No work notes exist for Stephanie Acosta—you must check your inbox directly to review her correspondence before the meeting. The team is actively opposing a massive $250 million IBM testing proposal—while simultaneously taking ownership of immediate tech decision approvals during this executive transition. You must also discuss the proposed elimination of low-value "one-pagers"—replacing them with self-service HTML fact sheets or simple one-liner notifications.
tasks: Search your email inbox for recent messages from Stephanie Acosta—clarify her specific requests before the sync begins.
Oppose the $250 million IBM testing proposal—citing the lack of defined use cases and the team's preference to build internally.
Establish interim guidelines for tech decision approvals—navigating the shift in ownership while the new executives onboard.
Advocate for the elimination of dense one-pagers—proposing a transition to HTML fact sheets and automated dashboard notifications.
613jvo8hikdmuscksfb3oqbk29.title: Quick Sync on Tech Decisions

2vn2lk2508i4mrm60n8144o74t.summary: You have tracked several critical workstreams across your last five bi-weekly meetings with Jonathan—most notably the reallocation of auto ecosystem responsibilities to Deepak and the strategic pivot of the paused IDP project. Your core architectural initiatives—Spartan, Snowflake sandbox integration, and agentic fulfillment—require clear next steps regarding data access and tooling strategy. You must resolve outstanding alignment issues with external stakeholders—specifically Brian Simpkin on Scan AI scope and Sima on chatbot patterns—before committing to the next delivery phase.
tasks: Transition LOB and auto ecosystem architectural responsibilities to Deepak—enabling his request for hands-on technical work.
Align with Kamali Kampati to merge the paused IDP questionnaire analysis into Phil Stackhouse's parallel project—saving resources.
Resolve the Scan AI scoping issues with Brian Simpkin—keeping the focus strictly on task mining.
Define the data access pathways for Project Spartan with Brian Osterkamp—unifying the codebase with the pipeline dashboards.
2vn2lk2508i4mrm60n8144o74t.title: bi-weekly 1on1 with Jonathan

262k656jmfk8gutj7tff7paroa.summary: You must prepare for the stand-up by focusing on the transition from legacy pricing to GuideWire—which has disrupted modeling processes—and the ongoing push under Suhas to consolidate process observability tools like Celonis and Skan.ai. Your previous discussions highlight critical operational shifts—such as moving 500 technology employees to Chris Vasquez's operations group and migrating O2 policies to Guidewire starting in Wisconsin. You also face unresolved data quality concerns and a lack of dedicated data architects—issues that continue to cause ripple effects in modeling and premium calculations.
tasks: Harden the manual processes—specifically the 20,000 custom Python scripts—into enterprise-grade IT assets under the new Pricing Technology Team.
Finalize the architecture position paper validating Celonis as the primary visualization layer for process observability.
Address the lack of a dedicated P&C data architect to stop the downstream modeling and premium errors.
Review the operational readiness of the claims team before the November two-way SMS pilot implementation.
262k656jmfk8gutj7tff7paroa.title: P&C IT Stand-up

1l6lc3m23de5n50d2tf27ctf98.summary: Review your May 7 discussion with Ryan Rist—the last documented sync—where you debated the "leapfrog" telematics strategy versus ancillary insurance products. Address the outstanding fifteen-million-dollar CMT pilot proposal and the competing product workbench pitches from Bain and Accenture. Resolve the downstream integration questions for Billing and FinnPal—unresolved since your March 16 sync—to ensure team agility.
tasks: Finalize the decision between the Tesla-style leapfrog telematics model and ancillary products—such as pet insurance—to clarify the incubator pathway.
Resolve the friction over the fifteen-million-dollar CMT pilot proposal by determining if direct-to-carrier OEM data is more viable.
Assess the competing product workbench pitches from Bain and Accenture to centralize product data.
Establish the downstream integration plan for Billing and FinnPal to prevent manual back-office bottlenecks.
1l6lc3m23de5n50d2tf27ctf98.title: Bi-Monthly EA Sync with Ryan Rist
